data.title: SentinelOne tumbles even as company ups guidance after Q3 results
data.description:
• SentinelOne (NYSE:S) shares fell 10% in late trading on Wednesday even as the cybersecurity company raised its guidance for fiscal 2025 after it reported third-quarter results that came in-line with estimates.
  • For fiscal 2025, SentinelOne said it now sees revenue of $818M, up from a previous view of $815M. Analysts were expecting $815.9M in revenue.
  • “For the first time, we delivered positive free cash flow on a trailing-twelve-month basis, a key milestone in our journey toward sustained profitability,” Barbara Larson, CFO of SentinelOne said in a statement. “Based on strong execution and business momentum, we’re raising our revenue growth outlook to 32% for the fiscal year ’25.”
  • For the period ending October 31, SentinelOne said it lost $0.25 per share as revenue rose 28% year-over-year to come in at $210.6M. Annual recurring revenue during the period came in at $859.7M, above the $857.3M estimate.
  • Analysts were expecting the company to lose $0.20 per share on $209.72M in revenue.
  • “Our Q3 results demonstrate strong execution and business momentum. We exceeded our topline growth expectations and re-accelerated new business growth,” said Tomer Weingarten, CEO of SentinelOne. “Enterprises are increasingly selecting Singularity Platform for real-time, autonomous security. With our industry-leading innovations and broadening platform capabilities, Singularity is setting the standard for the future of AI-powered cybersecurity.”
  • Looking ahead, SentinelOne expects to generate $222M in revenue for the fourth-quarter.
  • The company will hold a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: bluebird bio announces 1-for-20 reverse stock split; shares tumble
data.description:
• bluebird bio (NASDAQ:BLUE) announced that it will proceed with a 1-for-20 reverse stock split.
  • The primary goal of the reverse stock split is to increase the per-share market price of the company’s common stock to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement for continued listing.
  • The reverse stock split is expected to become effective at 5 p.m., Eastern Time, on December 12, 2024.
  • The common stock is expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the markets open on December 13, 2024.
  • BLUE shares slip nearly 33% in after hours trading on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Dow ends above 45,000, Nasdaq, S&P finish at new highs powered by tech rally
data.description:
Major equity averages ended at record highs Wednesday, fronted by climbing shares for Salesforce (CRM) and other tech companies, while Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made upbeat remarks about the world's largest economy.

Powell at a New York Times event said the U.S. economy is "strong," and was stronger than monetary policymakers had anticipated in September. "We can afford to be a little more cautious" in figuring out where the neutral rate is — the point at which the policy rate neither boosts nor drags on economic activity, he said.

"Offsetting this to some extent was a statement from Powell that the bank can be more cautious as inflation comes in ‘a little higher’ - the market seemed not weigh those comments too heavily, though," Daniel Jones, investing group leader of Crude Value Insights, told Seeking Alpha. "That, or market participants are perceiving [a] combination of data to indicate that a soft landing is likely."

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +1.3% for a record closing high. The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.6% for a record close at 6,086.49. The Dow (DJI) +0.7%, finishing above the 45,000 mark for the first time.

The Information Technology sector on the S&P 500 (SP500) +1.8%, pacing five other rising sectors. Five groups fell, with Energy losing -2.5%. The Magnificent 7 group of tech behemoths advanced, with Nvidia (NVDA) +3.5% and Amazon (AMZN) +2.2% but Meta (META) slipped from an all-time high.

Salesforce (CRM) was another standout in Wednesday trade, climbing 11% to top both the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Dow (DJI). The customer relations management software maker delivered somewhat mixed Q3 results, but investors cheered deal progress for its Agentforce AI enterprise system.

AI demand and prospects also helped power shares of Marvell (MRVL) +23% after the semiconductor company reported fiscal Q3 results and guidance that topped expectations.

Identity management company Okta (OKTA) +5.4% after its Q3 results and outlook surpassed expectations.

The Federal Reserve had flagged a slowdown in the job market as a factor in jump-starting its rate-cutting cycle this year. Early Wednesday, ADP's U.S. private nonfarm payrolls tracker came in showing a rise of 146K in November, short of consensus.

Investors slightly pushed up odds of a December rate cut, to 77.5% from ~73% on Tuesday, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

In Treasuries, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 4.19%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 4.14%.

Elsewhere on the economic front, the S&P U.S. Composite and Services PMI was revised down in November to 54.9.

The ISM Services PMI slipped more than expected in November to 52.1.

data.title: eHealth started at neutral by UBS, MA uncertainty cited
data.description:
UBS initiated coverage of eHealth (NASDAQ:EHTH) with a neutral rating, citing uncertainty in the Medicare Advantage space.

UBS said that while eHealth (EHTH) had “executed well” in improving core operations and cash flow, “it is difficult to have clear visibility into how the business will perform, in the near term, give an expectation of elevated churn across the industry.”

The investment bank added that if the Medicare Advantage industry stabilizes down the road, it could get more constructive.

UBS set its price target for the stock at $5.50.

data.title: nCino Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.05, revenue of $138.8M beats by $1.42M
data.description:
• nCino press release (NASDAQ:NCNO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $138.8M (+13.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.42M.

  • Financial Outlook

  • nCino is providing guidance for its fourth quarter ending January 31, 2025, as follows:

  • Total revenues between $139.5 million and $141.5 million.

  • Subscription revenues between $122.5 million and $124.5 million.

  • Non-GAAP operating income between $23.25 million and $24.25 million.

  • Non-GAAP net income attributable to nCino per diluted share of $0.18 to $0.19.

  • nCino is providing guidance for its fiscal year 2025 ending January 31, 2025, as follows:

  • Total revenues between $539.0 million and $541.0 million.
  • Subscription revenues between $467.0 million and $469.0 million.
  • Non-GAAP operating income between $95.0 million and $96.0 million.
  • Non-GAAP net income attributable to nCino per diluted share of $0.75 to $0.76.

data.title: Torrid becomes the latest retailer to suffer consumer snub
data.description:
Torrid (NYSE:CURV) shares are circling the drain near the overnight low as a variety of factors including the election, a more discerning consumer, two hurricanes in October, and a lack of new offerings were blamed for a very disappointing quarter and an underwhelming outlook for the current quarter and remainder of the year.

The results echo similar sentiments from other retailers that have seen sales and profitability hurt by the compromised economic environment and consumers become more discriminating on how they spend fewer discretionary dollars.

The company’s CEO acknowledge the company’s shortcomings last quarter, namely the lack of “newness and novelty” in the fall core collection.

“We saw softness in our core collection, which was not innovative enough in an environment where consumer spending was constrained,” CEO Lisa Harper said on the company’s earnings call, adding that Torrid now recognizes that the core product was too similar to what had been offered, and did not infuse it with enough freshness and fashion. Said differently, “It was too much of the same thing she already had in her closet.”

“The new assortment could provide life to the brand and potentially lift comparable sales and leverage in 2025,” Seeking Alpha analyst Quipus Capital writes. But even with a 30% reversal, the stock is still not priced attractively, at least not until Q1 20225 when the new assortments potentially revive the brand.

“Although Torrid stock is cheaper than it has ever been since [April 2024] I now only believe the name is barely fairly priced, but not an opportunity. Below $3, and particularly below $2.50, I would become much more interested in Torrid,” Quipus Capital adds.

This would imply another 17% decline in the stock, on top of the 27% drop since Tuesday’s close.

Related tickers: Kohl's (KSS), Target (TGT), Guess? (GES), Shoe Carnival (SCVL)

data.title: Five Below Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.25, revenue of $843.7M beats by $42.72M
data.description:
• Five Below press release (NASDAQ:FIVE): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.25.
  • Revenue of $843.7M (+14.6% Y/Y) beats by $42.72M.

  • For the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2024:

  • Net sales are expected to be in the range of $1.35 billion to $1.38 billion based on opening approximately 22 net new stores and assumes an approximate 3% to 5% decrease in comparable sales.

  • Net income is expected to be in the range of $174 million to $184 million. Adjusted net income(3) is expected to be in the range of $179 million to $189 million.

  • Diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $3.15 to $3.33 on approximately 55.3 million diluted weighted average shares outstanding. Adjusted diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $3.23 to $3.41.

  • For the full year of Fiscal 2024:

  • Net sales are expected to be in the range of $3.84 billion to $3.87 billion based on opening approximately 227 net new stores and assumes an approximate 3% decrease in comparable sales.
  • Net income is expected to be in the range of $240 million to $250 million. Adjusted net income is expected to be in the range of $265 million to $275 million.
  • Diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $4.34 to $4.52 on approximately 55.3 million diluted weighted average shares outstanding. Adjusted diluted income per common share is expected to be in the range of $4.78 to $4.96.
  • Gross capital expenditures are expected to be approximately $340 million in fiscal 2024.

data.title: 'We don't have any fears," Rio Tinto CEO says of incoming Trump administration
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) expects the upcoming change in U.S. political leadership to offer potential upside as well as downside, CEO Jakob Stausholm said Wednesday at the company's investor seminar in London.

"We don't have any fears," the CEO said, even after President-elect Trump recently rattled markets with talk of higher tariffs on all goods from Canada and China, noting the previous Trump administration was fruitful to the company.

Rio Tinto (RIO) produces most of its aluminum in Canada, while 14.5% of the company is owned by a Chinese state-owned aluminum firm.

Rio (RIO) shares wobble between small gains and losses in Wednesday's trading after forecasting little change in iron ore shipments from its Australian operations for 2025, while expecting Y/Y gains in copper and bauxite production.

While Rio's (RIO) profits flow mostly from iron ore, the miner is increasing its focus on copper, whose demand is expected to benefit from the energy transition.

Rio (RIO) is targeting copper production of 1M tons/year by the end of the decade, underpinned by rising output from the Oyu Tolgoi mine in Mongolia, where production is expected to jump by more than 50% next year.

Citi analysts say Rio's (RIO) guidance update looks largely in line with expectations, with iron ore shipment targets seen coming in flat next year - slightly lower than Citi's forecast - while Rio sees $11B in capital spending for 2025, up from $9.5B in 2024 and $800M more than Citi had expected.

data.title: GoDaddy gets up after showing off Airo monetization strategy
data.description:
GoDaddy (NYSE:GDDY) shares moved higher Wednesday after the web hosting company showcased the monetization strategy behind Airo Plus, its artificial intelligence-powered web building platform.

Shares climbed 4% by late afternoon trading. GoDaddy held an investor dinner on Tuesday that detailed a new $5 per month subscription plan for Airo Plus. The subscription provides access to various functionalities, including a premium logo maker, insights around the user's site and social media.

GoDaddy claims Airo Plus can design a website, logo and create an email and social campaign in the time it takes to read a single sentence.

"We took initial pricing as a reflection of feedback the company has seen from customer testing, but we believe that between the value being provided, combined with the additional tools which can drive further attach of other products, the value creation from Airo Plus could be substantial over time, and we'd think the monthly price could move up accordingly," said RBC analyst Brad Erickson, in an investor note on Wednesday.

Following the presentation, RBC increased its price target on the stock to $230 from $190 and maintained its Outperform rating.

"Airo Plus as a premium offering shows a clearer path to monetization, and while we think it’ll take some time to show up in numbers, the low initial price​-​point for a number of features will likely see decent uptake," Barclays analysts said.
